title: Patients Are Sending Digital Signals. Are Companies Listening?
gist: Pharma and medtech brands lose prospective patients because fragmented digital signals never become coordinated next steps, and tightening privacy rules are eroding the third-party data legacy acquisition depended on. BCG argues agentic AI tying together predictive and generative AI into a real-time decisioning layer over unified first-party data delivers large conversion and ROI gains.

insights.claim: Most patients now begin their health care journey online, yet most direct-to-patient programs still run on predetermined sequences and broad segments whose cadence never changes based on patient behavior.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Static DTP models are leaking conversions, creating a market for real-time decisioning vendors in healthcare marketing.
insights.quote: Many DTP models still run on predetermined sequences and broad segments whose cadence never changes based on what the patient is actually doing.

insights.claim: Industrywide, only about 2% of brands have reached individual-customer-level, cross-channel data-driven marketing.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: A vast addressable gap exists for AI-driven personalization tooling in pharma and medtech marketing.
insights.quote: only about 2% of brands have reached a level of data-driven marketing where decisions are made at the individual customer level across channels
insights.locator: The organizational dimension makes coordination harder.

insights.claim: The bottleneck in patient marketing is rarely the platform but the absence of a shared patient data model and a journey roadmap connecting capability to measurable outcomes.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Investment should target data unification and activation, not more technology platforms.
insights.quote: Technology investment consistently outpaces activation. The bottleneck is rarely the platform; it is the absence of a shared patient data model

insights.claim: As data regulations tighten, broad-tracking tools become harder and more expensive to scale, making first-party consent-based data the most defensible conversion asset a health care brand can develop because it compounds rather than depreciates.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: First-party data assets become a durable competitive moat; brands dependent on third-party tracking face rising costs.
insights.quote: First-party, consent-based data——built directly from patient engagement——is now the most defensible conversion asset a health care brand can develop, because it compounds over time rather than depreciating.
insights.locator: Privacy concerns are reshaping acquisition economics.

themes.theme: The three-layer AI stack for conversion

insights.claim: Predictive AI scores each patient's likelihood of converting or dropping out, generative AI produces MLR-compliant content variants at scale, and agentic AI is the execution layer that decides and acts on the next best action while feeding outcomes back into the patient profile.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Conversion value accrues to whoever orchestrates the three AI layers into a self-improving loop, not to any single tool.
insights.quote: Agentic AI is the execution layer. It observes real-time signals, decides the next best action for each patient (channel, timing, content variant, or escalation to a human) and acts.
insights.locator: AI in patient marketing is not a single tool.

insights.claim: Most health care organizations already have predictive or generative AI somewhere in production, but the conversion gains come only when agentic AI ties them together into a continuously improving system.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Agentic orchestration is the missing layer where the next wave of healthcare AI value will be captured.
insights.quote: The conversion gains come when agentic AI ties them together into a system that continuously improves.

insights.claim: A BCG analysis in health care provider systems benchmarked AI-driven personalized digital marketing at roughly six times return on marketing spend and a 20% incremental revenue lift when underlying data and decisioning are in place.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Concrete ROI benchmark supports the investment case for unified-data AI marketing in healthcare.
insights.quote: roughly six times return on marketing spend and a 20% incremental revenue lift when the underlying data and decisioning are in place
insights.locator: The upside is visible in the results.

insights.claim: At a major medtech company, an AI effort to convert leads into loyal customers delivered a 10% reduction in cost per lead and a six-point lift in lead conversion rate in the first year.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Demonstrates near-term, first-year payback from AI lead conversion programs.
insights.quote: a 10% reduction in cost per lead and a six-point lift in lead conversion rate
insights.locator: Recent client engagements confirm the pattern.

insights.claim: An AI-powered activation program in cardiac monitoring-device adherence produced an 85% lift in patient-reported activation and reversed a multiyear decline in 120-day device return rates.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Shows AI activation improves clinical adherence and program economics, not just marketing metrics.
insights.quote: an 85% lift in patient-reported activation and reversed a multiyear decline in 120-day device return rates
insights.locator: In a separate engagement focused on cardiac monitoring

insights.claim: The fastest route to better patient conversion is a focused four-to-six-week diagnostic that quantifies funnel drop-off and aligns on two or three priority journeys scored by impact versus effort, rather than rebuilding everything.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: A low-cost diagnostic-first approach lowers the barrier to entry and de-risks AI adoption for healthcare leaders.
insights.quote: A focused four-to-six-week diagnostic should do five things
insights.locator: Commercial and digital leaders do not need

insights.claim: A minimum viable foundation for patient conversion needs five elements, and having three of the five in place is enough to begin a focused pilot, with the first AI-led journey generally measurable within eight to twelve weeks of build kickoff.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Single-quarter measurable results lower the investment threshold versus multiyear transformations.
insights.quote: The first AI-led direct-to-patient journey is generally measurable within eight to twelve weeks of build kickoff
insights.locator: For chief medical officers who are deciding

insights.claim: Creativity and media spend still matter but no longer determine the ceiling on patient conversion; fragmented signals do, and unifying them turns scattered interactions into conversion leverage.
insights.speaker: https://www.bcg.com/about/people/experts/alex-baxter
insights.so_what: Reframes the competitive battleground in healthcare marketing from creative spend to data integration.
insights.quote: Creativity and media spend still matter, but they no longer determine the ceiling on patient conversion. Fragmented signals do.
